note: Menu setting map harvested from BT-01 relay settings-walk captures (write 08 <sub> <val> tail; read 04 05/06/09 before/after; isolate the changed byte). payloadOffset = byte index in the full read frame INCLUDING the 2-byte `04 0X` header (so first data byte = offset 2); equals the harvested PROTOCOL.md byte[N]. blockOffset = payloadOffset - 2. value = literal option index (first option / off = 0). ACK status byte[3] = 00 on success. Every 08 write appends the constant writeTail below.
writeTailNote: 20-byte fixed context payload, byte-identical across all observed 08 writes (key tone .. NR .. VOX .. color). v1 can emit it verbatim.

05.role: core settings A (the big one — most menu items)

06.role: settings B — audio gains + display font color (band/step table region)

09.role: settings C — BT-01 gain, noise reduction, GPS, channel colors, VOX, fan, mic

settings.name: key_tone
settings.desc: Beep volume for keypad and button presses.

settings.name: digital_idle
settings.desc: Comfort tone played while idle on a DMR channel.

settings.name: analog_idle
settings.desc: Enable the analog idle / roger tone.

settings.name: startup_sound
settings.desc: Play the boot chime when the radio powers on.

settings.name: talk_permit
settings.desc: Which modes require the channel to be free before transmit is allowed.

settings.name: d_reset_tone
settings.desc: Play a tone when the DMR call hang-timer resets.

settings.name: mic_speaker_set
settings.desc: Route the external mic's speaker audio to the Main or Sub side.

settings.name: max_volume_level
settings.desc: Upper limit for the master volume control.

settings.name: enhanced_sound
settings.desc: Audio / speech-enhancement profile for the speaker.

settings.name: sms_notify
settings.desc: Alert when an incoming SMS text message arrives.

settings.name: call_ring
settings.desc: Ring tone when a private or group call comes in.

settings.name: digital_mic_level
settings.desc: Microphone gain used on digital (DMR) transmit.

settings.name: analog_mic_level
settings.desc: Microphone gain used on analog (FM) transmit.

settings.name: noise_reduction_rx
settings.desc: Digital noise reduction applied to received audio.

settings.name: noise_reduction_tx
settings.desc: Digital noise reduction applied to transmitted audio.

settings.name: back_light
settings.desc: Display and keypad backlight brightness.

settings.name: backlight_time
settings.desc: How long the backlight stays lit after the last key press.

settings.name: channel_display
settings.desc: Show the channel by name/number or by its frequency.

settings.name: menu_exit_time
settings.desc: Idle time before the on-radio menu closes itself.

settings.name: change_font_color
settings.desc: Text color used on the channel display.

settings.name: channel_color_b
settings.desc: Accent color for the Sub (B) channel.

settings.name: channel_color_a
settings.desc: Accent color for the Main (A) channel.

settings.name: sub_channel
settings.desc: Enable the second receiver (dual watch). Off leaves a single active side.

settings.name: auto_power_off
settings.desc: Automatically power the radio off after this idle period.

settings.name: tx_timer
settings.desc: Time-out timer — the maximum continuous transmit length.

settings.name: tot_predict
settings.desc: Sound a warning shortly before the transmit time-out timer expires.

settings.name: activate_fan
settings.desc: When the cooling fan is allowed to run.

settings.name: frequency_step
settings.desc: VFO tuning step size.

settings.name: analog_squelch_level
settings.desc: Squelch threshold for analog (FM) receive.

settings.name: tbst_select
settings.desc: Tone-burst frequency used for repeater access.

settings.name: vox_level
settings.desc: Voice-activated transmit (VOX) sensitivity.

settings.name: vox_delay
settings.desc: Hang time before VOX drops transmit after you stop talking.

settings.name: scan_mode
settings.desc: How a scan resumes: Time-operated, Carrier-operated, or Search.

settings.name: dtmf_speed
settings.desc: DTMF tone timing (send rate) for auto-dial.

settings.name: bt_mic_gain
settings.desc: Gain of the Bluetooth (BT-01 / hands-free) microphone into the radio. Ported from the PoC (raw 0-4 = display 1-5).

settings.name: bt_speaker_gain
settings.desc: Level of radio audio sent to the Bluetooth speaker path. Ported from the PoC (raw 0-4 = display 1-5).

settings.name: bt01_speaker_gain
settings.desc: The BT-01 remote head's own speaker level. Ported from the PoC (raw 0-4 = display 1-5).

settings.name: digi_monitor
settings.desc: DMR promiscuous monitor: hear other talkgroups on the current (single) or both (double) time slots. Read offset pinned in the PoC.

settings.name: gps
settings.desc: GPS receiver power. Ported from the PoC (writes 08 3c..3e are contiguous with DigiMon 3f).

settings.name: gps_mode
settings.desc: Satellite constellation selection. Ported from the PoC.

settings.name: gps_area_sql
settings.desc: GPS area squelch (only open squelch inside a configured area). Ported from the PoC.

settings.name: external_audio_jack
settings.desc: Route received audio to the rear external-speaker jack. Write 08 46 <0|1> (BT-01 relay capture 2026-07-12); reads back from settings block 04 06 byte 72 (found by diffing full startup enumerations with the jack on vs off).

open.name: power_save
open.detail: toggled both directions with NO 08 write and NO diff in any of 05/06/09 (only 5a 0d menu-nav frames). Lives elsewhere (different block, a bit not in these reads, or a non-08 command). Needs a wider read or a different walk.

open.name: language
open.status: single-option (English) on this firmware — no diff to confirm; op 08 17 observed but value unchanged.

open.name: channel_color_a (op 08 33)
open.detail: op 08 33 produced NO diff (value 06 written twice with no change). 08 35 is the one that cleanly drives block 09[54]. 08 33 may be a separate color field (channel-name color?) not in 05/06/09, or was a no-op repeat. Re-walk 08 33 from a known-different value.

open.name: vox_level (first capture)
open.status: bad capture (mislabelled TBST values) — superseded by the clean L3->L9 sample.

open.name: am_fm_radio
open.detail: 04 09 read timed out mid-walk; no data. Re-capture (and note: enabling AM/FM may change the radio's response timing).
